title: calophyllum drouhardii
intro: Calophyllum drouhardii, also known as the Tahina or Pacific Calophyllum, is a species of tropical tree native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in the island of Tahiti. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Calophyllum drouhardii include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Calophyllum drouhardii can be prepared into herbal remedies, including oil, tea, and tincture, which can be used externally or internally to harness its therapeutic properties.
